children.text: BankAxept is the Norwegian local card payment scheme and is the most widely accepted card for point of sale payments. Payments are approved in real time through the use of a chip and PIN, using contactless NFC technology or by mobile phone. The majority of BankAxept cards are co-branded. From these co-branded cards, almost all cards are co-branded with Visa and some with Mastercard. To process BankAxept payments, Adyen works with Nets, the company that operates the BankAxept scheme.

excerpt: Norway’s domestic card scheme for in-person payments, commonly used with chip & PIN and contactless.

content_features.title: Domestic coverage in Norway
content_features.description: Accept BankAxept for in-person transactions where Norwegian domestic cards are commonly used.

content_features.title: Real-time authorization
content_features.description: Authorizations are typically performed online and approved in real time, helping you confirm payment instantly at the point of sale.

content_features.title: Approval rate visibility
content_features.description: Review BankAxept approval rates and issuer decline patterns by market, card type, and currency before changing routing or retry rules.

content_features.title: Manage declines to improve approval rates
content_features.description: Track decline codes and routing results; optimizing terminal setup and transaction data can reduce avoidable declines and improve authorization success.

content_features.title: Multi-currency settlement
content_features.description: BankAxept supports settlement across multiple currencies depending on partner or acquirer setup.

content_features.title: Dispute monitoring
content_features.description: Monitor disputes and chargeback trends for BankAxept by reason code, issuer, and market before broad operational changes.

content_features.title: Reliability checks
content_features.description: Track decline spikes, issuer response patterns, and processor availability to isolate BankAxept performance issues faster.

tip:
If you see elevated declines, review whether co-branded cards are being routed as BankAxept vs.
international scheme and compare approval rates by route to spot configuration issues.

faq.question: What is BankAxept?
faq.answer: BankAxept is Norway’s domestic card payment scheme commonly used for point-of-sale card payments.

faq.question: Where can I offer BankAxept?
faq.answer: It is primarily relevant for in-person acceptance in Norway; it is a country-specific payment method.

faq.question: Why do some BankAxept payments get declined?
faq.answer: Declines can be caused by issuer decisions, card limits, offline/online authorization conditions, or how a co-branded card is routed (domestic vs international scheme). Reviewing decline codes and routing outcomes helps identify patterns.

faq.question: Are BankAxept cards co-branded with Visa or Mastercard?
faq.answer: Many BankAxept cards are co-branded—most commonly with Visa, and some with Mastercard—so the same physical card can support multiple scheme routes.

faq.question: How should I handle disputes for BankAxept transactions?
faq.answer: Handle disputes according to the applicable card scheme rules for the transaction. For in-person payments, keeping strong evidence (e.g., receipt and EMV data) can help when responding.

faq.question: How should merchants monitor BankAxept disputes?
faq.answer: Track BankAxept disputes, refunds, and routing outcomes by domestic versus international card path before adjusting processing rules.

faq.question: Does BankAxept support multiple currencies?
faq.answer: BankAxept is primarily a Norwegian domestic debit network and is commonly presented in NOK, while any settlement flexibility depends on your PSP.

seo.seo_description: Accept BankAxept, Norway’s domestic card scheme, for in-person payments including chip & PIN and contactless.
seo.keywords: BankAxept,Norway,domestic card scheme,in-person payments,contactless,chip and PIN
